rubric.evidence: conformance (wasm, headless): 14/14 auto-checkable pass. Real full-lifecycle run in headless Chromium with a persistent profile: first visit showed state=download-required + Download button; after downloading ~85 MB, AutoModelForMaskedLM produced REAL predictions for the default snippet 'def <mask>(numbers): return sum(numbers) / len(numbers)' → #1 ' sum' 38.1% (logit 13.45), then ' len', ' numbers', ' digits', ' number', ' count', ' mean', ' digit' — sensible function-name candidates given the body. 0 console errors. A separate low-level check confirmed a real MLM head: logits dims [1, 13, 52000] (full-vocab distribution), NOT feature-extraction.
rubric.notes: CodeBERTa is Hugging Face's CodeBERT-family RoBERTa masked-LM pre-trained on source CODE (CodeSearchNet: Go/Java/JS/PHP/Python/Ruby). Distinct domain from the general/clinical/biomedical fill-mask demos. The originally-requested Xenova/codebert-base-mlm has no ONNX export; onnx-community/CodeBERTa-small-v1-ONNX is the runnable code-MLM with a real head.

rubric.evidence: conformance: io-shape-semantic=manual. A real run produces ranked code-token candidates for the masked position with softmax probabilities AND the exact pre-softmax logits (read from the [1,seq,52000] tensor at the mask row). Byte-level-BPE leading-space tokens (Ġ) are surfaced as ␣ so ' sum' reads correctly. Real sample above; the top-1 fills into the completed snippet.
rubric.notes: Multi-mask supported; entropy of the top-k is shown as a confidence gauge.

rubric.evidence: conformance: declares-model-id (onnx-community/CodeBERTa-small-v1-ONNX, linked to card), declares-task (fill-mask), declares-backend (WASM), declares-quantisation (q8), inference-off-main-thread (Web Worker: codefill.js CodeFillEngine → worker.js) all pass. At-a-glance also documents the RoBERTa arch, 52k code vocab, and the CodeSearchNet training set.
rubric.notes: License recorded honestly (no explicit card license; weights public + ungated; CodeSearchNet data MIT).

rubric.evidence: conformance: shared-auto-init-loader=pass; auto-init-on-cached=manual, VERIFIED: after the first-visit download, a same-profile reload auto-initialised to state=ready with NO Download button (downloadButton=false) and 0 console errors; the page auto-ran the default snippet on ready. First visit correctly showed the Download control.
rubric.notes: Uses shared createModelLoader; no hand-rolled Load button.

rubric.evidence: conformance: live-status-region, errors-on-the-page, runs-without-webgpu-fallback all pass + honest-progress-retry-offline=manual. Download progress renders in the shared loader; a missing <mask> shows a readable status prompt; prediction failures surface as an .err status; createModelLoader provides the Retry/recovery path. WASM model needs no WebGPU.

conformance: controls-operable=manual. Verified controls: language-starter buttons (Python/JS/Java/Go/PHP/Ruby) with aria-pressed; the code textarea (auto-predicts on input, debounced); Insert <mask> button; top-k range slider with live value; clicking a predicted token fills it into the code. On ready the page auto-predicted and rendered the ranked list above.
rubric.notes: All controls are real button/input/textarea elements with visible labels + focus-visible outlines.

rubric.evidence: conformance: no-fake-output=manual. Every prediction is decoded from the model's real logits at the mask position; nothing is hardcoded. The tokenization 'see inside' view is computed live from the real tokenizer. A missing mask shows a prompt rather than a fake result.

conformance: images-have-alt=pass (no ; inline SVG data-URI icon), buttons-have-names=pass. Language picker uses role=group + aria-pressed; status uses role=status aria-live; prediction rows are real buttons with token text; every field has a
rubric.notes: Applied accessibility guidance: aria-pressed language toggles, accessible names, live status.

rubric.evidence: conformance: responsive-desktop=pass, responsive-mobile=pass; parity-both-classes=manual. responsive-check.mjs: desktop (1280×800) clean, mobile (360×740 DPR3) clean — no horizontal overflow. Agent read screenshots (w17shots + reports/responsive/screens/codebert-fill-mask/): light + dark both legible; the prediction grid collapses columns and hides the logit column under 560px (media query); the code textarea and tokenized chips scroll/ wrap within their own boxes so long lines never break the page.
rubric.notes: responsive-check flags 4–11 'sub-24px' targets — inline crumb/reference text links, not primary tap targets; control buttons are ≥~34px (min-block-size 2.1rem).

rubric.evidence: conformance: inp-stays-low=manual. Inference runs in a Web Worker (CodeFillEngine → worker.js); the live-predict handler is debounced (400 ms) so keystrokes don't queue runs; the top-k partial-sort avoids sorting the whole 52k vocab.
rubric.notes: Applied identify-inp-causes / break-up-long-tasks: off-main-thread inference + debounced input + bounded top-k.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use flexbox with flex-wrap for control rows; set min-inline-size:0 / overflow-x:auto on items with long unbreakable content (code); use grid with a mobile column collapse for dense tables.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — language/scenario pickers and readouts are flex-wrap rows; the code textarea and .filled-code use overflow-x:auto (white-space:pre) so long code lines scroll inside their box, not the page; the prediction grid drops to fewer columns + hides the logit column below 560px; token chips wrap with word-break.

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Expose accessible names on all controls; use aria-pressed for toggle/segmented buttons; announce async state in a role=status aria-live region.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — language/scenario pickers are aria-pressed button groups; prediction rows are labelled buttons; run/prediction status is announced via role=status aria-live=polite.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance buttons-have-names=pass; aria-pressed toggling on the language picker verified in the headless run

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy JS off the main thread and keep interaction handlers short to protect INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — model download + masked-LM inference run in a Web Worker; the input handler is debounced so keystrokes don't spawn overlapping runs.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: conformance inference-off-main-thread=pass; worker.js runs AutoModelForMaskedLM off-thread

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Break up / bound long main-thread loops so they don't spike INP.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — top-k is a bounded partial-sort over the 52k logits (no full sort); DOM render is O(top-k) rows; tokenization view is O(tokens).
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js topkFromRow maintains a small ordered buffer instead of sorting the whole vocab

guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use cross-document view transitions for catalogue-to-page navigation.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inherited from public/styles.css @view-transition navigation:auto (invariant 6); use-case cards use the shared .model-card grid.

openQuestions: Would adding onnx-community/codebert-javascript-ONNX (a language-specialised MLM) as a NEW slug show the value of per-language code models, or is CodeBERTa's six-language coverage enough here?
Should the demo highlight where a REAL token scores far below the top-k (an anomaly/typo signal) as its own dedicated surface?
Is CodeBERTa-small's 6-layer size the right trade-off for the browser, or would a larger code-MLM (if one ships a browser ONNX with a real head) be worth the extra download?

followUpGoals.goal: Agent matrix pass for codebert-fill-mask is done (desktop real-output run + mobile/desktop light+dark screenshots read); support flipped to ok. Re-audit if the design system changes.

Add a NEW additive demo for a language-specialised code MLM (e.g. onnx-community/codebert-javascript-ONNX) to contrast per-language vs multi-language code models (new slug, never replacing this page).

summary: Revision-1 build critique for codebert-fill-mask (fill-mask, WASM q8, onnx-community/CodeBERTa-small-v1-ONNX), evidenced by a real headless full-lifecycle run: first-visit download → REAL code-token predictions ('def <mask>(numbers)…' → ' sum' 38.1%, ' mean', ' count' …) with exact logits from a genuine MLM head (dims [1,13,52000]); cached reload auto-inits with no Download button and 0 console errors. Distinct CODE-domain masked-LM (six languages) vs the general/clinical fill-mask demos. Conformance 14/14 auto-checkable pass + 9 manual-evidenced; responsive desktop+mobile clean, light+dark legible.
